title: monitoring — published macros
description: The monitoring module publishes ZERO framework macros. Every consumer touches the monitoring surface via injected services (MonitoringProviderManager, HealthCheckExecutor, AlertPolicyEvaluator) or the #[AsMonitoringListener] / #[AsMonitoringProvider] / #[AsHealthCheckStrategy] discovery paths.
rationale: Monitoring is a SERVER-SIDE OBSERVER. The consumer contract is: configure health checks → evaluator fires alerts → grouping opens incidents → providers get notified. There is no request-level state that needs to be surfaced as a Request macro (contrast with facility::activeFacility which caches a per-request model).
Adding a monitoring-specific macro (e.g. Request::currentIncident) would signal a design smell — monitoring events are ephemeral fire-then-notify artifacts, not persistent per-request state.

If a downstream module needs an ergonomic accessor (e.g. 'is any alert firing for my tenant?'), the answer is a repository method + a helper (see helpers.json), not a Request macro. The tenant scope is already established via BelongsToTenant.
